prompt_observations.prompt_text: How does red and near-infrared light fit into a home wellness routine?
prompt_observations.reason: The brand was not mentioned or recommended across observed surfaces, while the answers produced useful category framing.

surface_framing_summary.framing: Neutral ChatGPT answer frames red and near-infrared light as part of a calm home wellness routine, including relaxation, skincare, post-exercise recovery, screen-free reset moments and consistency.
observation_notes: Lower-risk routine framing focused on calm home wellness, relaxation, skincare, post-exercise recovery, screen-free reset moments and consistency.

surface_framing_summary.framing: Gemini frames red and near-infrared light as an at-home photobiomodulation wellness routine, with skincare, post-workout recovery and evening wind-down use cases.
observation_notes: Higher-risk physiological and scientific language appeared, including photobiomodulation, collagen production, cellular turnover, inflammation, tissue repair and melatonin.

surface_framing_summary.framing: Perplexity frames red and near-infrared light as a practical home wellness habit for skin support, post-exercise recovery, relaxation, safety-aware use and expectation management.
observation_notes: Practical setup, safety and benefit-related language appeared, including skin support, recovery, pregnancy and light-sensitive medications.

claim_risk_notes: Mentions post-exercise recovery and near-infrared light reaching deeper beneath the skin; keep as review-only and avoid converting into verified product claims.
No treatment, cure or guaranteed-result claim detected in this answer.

The answer uses higher-risk scientific and physiological claims including photobiomodulation, collagen production, cellular turnover, inflammation, tissue repair, muscle soreness, joint inflammation, circadian rhythms and melatonin production.

The answer states that NIR light is used for reducing inflammation and accelerating physical recovery, which should remain review-only and must not be converted into customer-facing product claims without claim validation and source review.
The answer includes strong benefit language such as biological benefits are cumulative and yields much better results; these should not be reused as verified claims.

The answer mentions muscle recovery, soreness, joint comfort, reduced redness, hair and scalp support, sleep and light-sensitive medications; these should remain review-only and must not be converted into verified product claims without claim validation and source review.
The answer includes safety-related guidance for eye conditions, skin conditions, pregnancy and light-sensitive medications; this should not be reused as medical advice.
The answer is relatively balanced because it says effects can be subtle and research is still evolving.

notes: Manual AI visibility observations are review-only and must not be treated as final visibility metrics, population-level findings, verified facts or customer-facing claims.
